name: Artichokes Jewish Style
recipeInstructions:
Trim the artichokes as detailed in "How to clean artichokes- Jewish style" and keep them in lemon water until you are ready to use them. Drain the first two artichokes, holding each by the stem and bottom, and gently tap the leafy parts against each other until one opens slightly. Place the opened artichoke, bottom up, on a board or working surface. Repeat with another artichoke and line it next to the first. Continue this process until all artichokes are opened. For the last artichoke, tap it against the board.
In a small bowl, combine salt and pepper. Sprinkle each artichoke, including between leaves, with the salt and pepper mixture.
Heat oil in a deep earthenware or similar saucepan so that artichokes fit in one layer over moderate heat for 20 to 25 minutes, or until bottoms and sides are well browned. During cooking, sprinkle cold water over the artichokes to produce steam, ensuring even cooking inside. To do this authentically, keep a bowl of cold water near the range; dip your closed fist in the water and open it forcefully over the roasting artichokes several times.
When done, transfer artichokes to a plate, bottom side down, to retain moisture. Pick up each at the bottom with a fork and briefly dip into hot oil again, pressing leaves against the pan's bottom. The artichokes will open like roses and leaves become golden and crisp. Serve immediately.
Although traditionally served piping hot, these are delicious at room temperature. Preparing several hours ahead is possible; if they dry out during reheating, sprinkle cold water over them before finishing with the oil.
